creator: Aisha Malik
title: (No AI) Google just declared itself a contender in AI design at IO 2026
dc:creator: Aisha Malik
content: Google says it's designed the app to be accessible to everyone, from teachers to small business owners.
contentSnippet: Google says it's designed the app to be accessible to everyone, from teachers to small business owners.

creator: Sarah Perez
title: (No AI) You can now talk to your Gmail inbox, as seen at Google IO 2026
dc:creator: Sarah Perez
content: Google expands Gmail’s AI Inbox with conversational voice search, letting users ask Gemini to find buried email details.
contentSnippet: Google expands Gmail’s AI Inbox with conversational voice search, letting users ask Gemini to find buried email details.

title: (No AI) Demis Hassabis said this might be the ‘foothills of the singularity.’ What?
author: Stevie Bonifield
content:
![Google DeepMind CEO Demis Hassabis on stage during the Google I/O 2026 keynote](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/05/Demis_GDM.OMNI_.AI-in-Real-World_3.jpg?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

Welcome to a "profound moment for humanity," according to Google DeepMind CEO Demis Hassabis, who closed out [Google I/O's keynote presentation](https://www.youtube.com/live/wYSncx9zLIU?t=10459s) on Tuesday, saying:

> Google's cutting-edge research and products will help unlock AGI's incredible potential for the benefit of the entire world. When we look back at this time, I think we will realize that we were standing in the foothills of the singularity.
> 
> It will be a profound moment for humanity. This technology will be a force multiplier for human ingenuity and usher in a new golden age of scientific discovery and progress, improving the lives of everyone, everywhere. We look forward to bu …

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/tech/934260/google-io-ai-singularity-demis-hassabis)
contentSnippet:
Welcome to a "profound moment for humanity," according to Google DeepMind CEO Demis Hassabis, who closed out Google I/O's keynote presentation on Tuesday, saying:
Google's cutting-edge research and products will help unlock AGI's incredible potential for the benefit of the entire world. When we look back at this time, I think we will realize that we were standing in the foothills of the singularity. 
It will be a profound moment for humanity. This technology will be a force multiplier for human ingenuity and usher in a new golden age of scientific discovery and progress, improving the lives of everyone, everywhere. We look forward to bu …

Read the full story at The Verge.
summary: Welcome to a "profound moment for humanity," according to Google DeepMind CEO Demis Hassabis, who closed out Google I/O's keynote presentation on Tuesday, saying: Google's cutting-edge research and products will help unlock AGI's incredible potential for the benefit of the entire world. When we look back at this time, I think we will realize that […]

title: (No AI) The future of Google is a search box that does everything
author: Jay Peters
content:
![](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/05/lcimg-036c9a25-7169-4853-846e-94fd035c259f.jpeg?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

Last year, after watching Google's I/O keynote, I wrote that it felt like [Google's future was Google googling](https://www.theverge.com/google/671200/google-googling-ai-mode-project-mariner-i-o-2025). After watching this year's I/O keynote on Tuesday, I don't think Google just wants to google for you - I think it wants to do _everything_ for you, all from a search box.

Take the trusty [Google search bar itself](https://www.theverge.com/tech/932970/google-search-ai-update-io-2026), something Google is generally hesitant to update, which is getting some updates. It will "dynamically" expand as you type longer queries. It will offer "AI-powered suggestions" that Google claims will "go beyond autocomplete," which could cause you to fill in the blanks of a search in a way you didn't intend and that may or …

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/tech/934217/google-search-box-does-everything-ai-io-2026)

Take the trusty Google search bar itself, something Google is generally hesitant to update, which is getting some updates. It will "dynamically" expand as you type longer queries. It will offer "AI-powered suggestions" that Google claims will "go beyond autocomplete," which could cause you to fill in the blanks of a search in a way you didn't intend and that may or  …
Read the full story at The Verge.

title: (No AI) Introducing OpenAI for Singapore
content: OpenAI for Singapore launches a multi-year AI partnership to expand deployment, build local talent, and support businesses and public services with AI.
contentSnippet: OpenAI for Singapore launches a multi-year AI partnership to expand deployment, build local talent, and support businesses and public services with AI.

title: (No AI) Advancing content provenance for a safer, more transparent AI ecosystem
content: OpenAI advances AI content provenance with Content Credentials, SynthID, and a verification tool to help people identify and trust AI-generated media.
contentSnippet: OpenAI advances AI content provenance with Content Credentials, SynthID, and a verification tool to help people identify and trust AI-generated media.

title: (No AI) Fast-tracking genetic leads to reverse cellular aging
content: Biologists use Co-Scientist to find novel factors that successfully rejuvenate human cells.
contentSnippet: Biologists use Co-Scientist to find novel factors that successfully rejuvenate human cells.

title: (No AI) Simulate real-world places with Project Genie and Street View
content: We’re expanding access to Google AI Ultra subscribers globally and introducing a new capability powered by Street View.
contentSnippet: We’re expanding access to Google AI Ultra subscribers globally and introducing a new capability powered by Street View.

creator: Miles Klee
title: (No AI) Literary Prizewinners Are Facing AI Allegations. It Feels Like the New Normal
dc:creator: Miles Klee
content: Three of five regional winners of the prestigious Commonwealth Short Story Prize are suspected of relying on chatbots. They’re certainly not alone.
contentSnippet: Three of five regional winners of the prestigious Commonwealth Short Story Prize are suspected of relying on chatbots. They’re certainly not alone.

creator: Boone Ashworth, Michael Calore
title: (No AI) Everything Announced at Google I/O 2026: Gemini, Search, Smart Glasses
dc:creator: Boone Ashworth, Michael Calore
content: Google is sprucing up its Gemini models, revamping search, and enabling AI agents in everything. There are also some spiffy new smart glasses coming this fall.
contentSnippet: Google is sprucing up its Gemini models, revamping search, and enabling AI agents in everything. There are also some spiffy new smart glasses coming this fall.
